let deleteButton dispatch (wt: WorktreeStatus) =
    Html.button [
        prop.className "delete-btn"
        prop.title "Remove worktree (Del)"
        yield! noFocusProps
        prop.onClick (fun e ->
            e.stopPropagation()
            dispatch (ConfirmDeleteWorktree wt.Branch))
        prop.children [ binIcon ]
    ]

let archiveButton dispatch = ArchiveViews.archiveButton (ArchiveMsg >> dispatch)

let prBadgeContent (repoName: string) (pr: PrInfo) =
    React.fragment [
        if pr.IsMerged then
            Interop.createElement "a" [
                prop.className "pr-badge merged"
                prop.title pr.Title
                prop.href pr.Url
                prop.target "_blank"
                prop.text "Merged"
            ]
        else
            Interop.createElement "a" [
                prop.className (if pr.IsDraft then "pr-badge draft" else "pr-badge")
                prop.title pr.Title
                prop.href pr.Url
                prop.target "_blank"
                prop.text ($"PR #{pr.Id}")
            ]
            match pr.Comments with
            | WithResolution (unresolved, total) when total > 0 ->
                Html.span [
                    prop.className (if unresolved = 0 then "thread-badge dimmed" else "thread-badge")
                    prop.text ($"{unresolved}/{total} threads")
                ]
            | CountOnly total ->
                Html.span [
                    prop.className (if total = 0 then "thread-badge dimmed" else "thread-badge")
                    prop.text ($"{total} comments")
                ]
            | _ -> ()
            buildBadges repoName pr.Builds
    ]

let prSection (repoName: string) (wt: WorktreeStatus) =
    match wt.Pr with
    | NoPr -> Html.none
    | HasPr pr -> prBadgeContent repoName pr

let prRow (repoName: string) (wt: WorktreeStatus) =
    match wt.Pr with
    | NoPr -> Html.none
    | HasPr pr ->
        Html.div [
            prop.className "pr-row"
            prop.children [ prBadgeContent repoName pr ]
        ]

let workMetricsView = ArchiveViews.workMetricsView

let compactWorktreeCard dispatch editorName (repoName: string) (scopedKey: string) (isFocused: bool) (wt: WorktreeStatus) =
    let baseClass = cardClassName wt + " compact"
    let className = if isFocused then baseClass + " focused" else baseClass
    Html.div [
        prop.key wt.Branch
        prop.className className
        prop.onClick (fun _ -> dispatch (SetFocus (Some (Card scopedKey))))
        prop.children [
            Html.div [
                prop.className "card-header"
                prop.children [
                    Html.span [ prop.className ($"ct-dot {ctClassName wt.CodingTool}") ]
                    Html.span [ prop.className "branch-name"; prop.text wt.Branch ]
                    workMetricsView wt.WorkMetrics
                    Html.span [ prop.className "commit-time"; prop.text (relativeTime System.DateTimeOffset.Now wt.LastCommitTime) ]
                    terminalButton dispatch wt
                    if wt.HasActiveSession then newTabButton dispatch wt
                    editorButton dispatch editorName wt
                    archiveButton dispatch wt
                    deleteButton dispatch wt
                ]
            ]
            Html.div [
                prop.className "compact-detail"
                prop.children [
                    if beadsTotal wt.Beads > 0 then beadsCounts "beads-inline" wt.Beads
                    mainBehindIndicator wt.MainBehindCount
                    prSection repoName wt
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]

let worktreeCard dispatch editorName (repoName: string) (branchEvents: CardEvent list) (isPending: bool) (scopedKey: string) (isFocused: bool) (wt: WorktreeStatus) =
    let baseClass = cardClassName wt
    let className = if isFocused then baseClass + " focused" else baseClass
    let hasContent = wt.LastUserMessage.IsSome || (not (List.isEmpty branchEvents))
    let footerClass = if hasContent then "card-footer has-content" else "card-footer"
    Html.div [
        prop.key wt.Branch
        prop.className className
        prop.onClick (fun _ -> dispatch (SetFocus (Some (Card scopedKey))))
        prop.children [
            Html.div [
                prop.className "card-body"
                prop.children [
                    Html.div [
                        prop.className "card-header"
                        prop.children [
                            Html.span [ prop.className ($"ct-dot {ctClassName wt.CodingTool}") ]
                            Html.span [ prop.className "branch-name"; prop.text wt.Branch ]
                            workMetricsView wt.WorkMetrics
                            terminalButton dispatch wt
                            if wt.HasActiveSession then newTabButton dispatch wt
                            editorButton dispatch editorName wt
                            archiveButton dispatch wt
                            deleteButton dispatch wt
                        ]
                    ]

                    if beadsTotal wt.Beads > 0 then
                        Html.div [
                            prop.className "beads-row"
                            prop.children [
                                beadsCounts "beads-counts" wt.Beads
                                beadsProgressBar wt.Beads
                            ]
                        ]

                    mainBehindWithSync dispatch wt branchEvents isPending scopedKey

                    prRow repoName wt
                ]
            ]

            Html.div [
                prop.className footerClass
                prop.children [
                    match wt.LastUserMessage with
                    | Some (prompt, ts) ->
                        Html.div [
                            prop.className "user-prompt"
                            prop.children [
                                Html.span [ prop.className "event-time"; prop.text (relativeEventTime ts) ]
                                Html.span [ prop.text prompt ]
                            ]
                        ]
                    | None -> ()

                    eventLog branchEvents
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]

let renderCard dispatch editorName isCompact (focusedElement: FocusTarget option) repoId repoName (branchEvents: Map<string, CardEvent list>) (syncPending: Set<string>) (wt: WorktreeStatus) =
    let scopedKey = $"{repoId}/{wt.Branch}"
    let events = branchEvents |> Map.tryFind scopedKey |> Option.defaultValue []
    let isPending = syncPending |> Set.contains scopedKey
    let isFocused = focusedElement = Some (Card scopedKey)
    if isCompact then compactWorktreeCard dispatch editorName repoName scopedKey isFocused wt
    else worktreeCard dispatch editorName repoName events isPending scopedKey isFocused wt

let archiveSection dispatch = ArchiveViews.archiveSection (ArchiveMsg >> dispatch)

let skeletonCard () =
    Html.div [
        prop.className "wt-card skeleton"
        prop.children [
            Html.div [
                prop.className "card-header"
                prop.children [
                    Html.span [ prop.className "skeleton-dot" ]
                    Html.span [ prop.className "skeleton-bar skeleton-branch" ]
                ]
            ]
            Html.div [ prop.className "skeleton-bar skeleton-commit" ]
            Html.div [ prop.className "skeleton-bar skeleton-beads" ]
        ]
    ]

    30→
    31→type Msg =
    32→    | DataLoaded of DashboardResponse
    33→    | DataFailed of exn
    34→    | ToggleSort
    35→    | ToggleCompact
    36→    | ToggleCollapse of repoId: RepoId
    37→    | Tick
    38→    | OpenTerminal of WorktreePath
    39→    | OpenEditor of WorktreePath
    40→    | StartSync of branch: string * scopedKey: string
    41→    | SyncStarted of key: string * Result<unit, string>
    42→    | SyncStatusUpdate of Map<string, CardEvent list>
    43→    | CancelSync of string
    44→    | SyncTick
    45→    | ConfirmDeleteWorktree of branch: string
    46→    | DeleteWorktree of string
    47→    | DeleteCompleted of Result<unit, string>
    48→    | FocusSession of path: WorktreePath
    49→    | OpenNewTab of path: WorktreePath
    50→    | SessionResult of Result<unit, string>
    51→    | KeyPressed of key: string * hasModifier: bool
    52→    | SetFocus of FocusTarget option
    53→    | ArchiveMsg of ArchiveViews.Msg
    54→    | ModalMsg of CreateWorktreeModal.Msg
    55→
    56→let worktreeApi =
    57→    Remoting.createApi ()
    58→    |> Remoting.buildProxy<IWorktreeApi>
    59→
    60→let fetchWorktrees () =
    61→    Cmd.OfAsync.either worktreeApi.getWorktrees () DataLoaded DataFailed
    62→
    63→let fetchSyncStatus () =
    64→    Cmd.OfAsync.perform worktreeApi.getSyncStatus () SyncStatusUpdate
    65→
    66→let hasSyncRunning (events: Map<string, CardEvent list>) =
    67→    events
    68→    |> Map.exists (fun _ evts ->
    69→        evts
    70→        |> List.exists (fun e ->
    71→            e.Status = Some StepStatus.Running))
    72→
    73→let init () =
    74→    { Repos = []
    75→      IsLoading = true
    76→      HasError = false
    77→      SortMode = ByActivity
    78→      IsCompact = false
    79→      SchedulerEvents = []
    80→      LatestByCategory = Map.empty
    81→      BranchEvents = Map.empty
    82→      SyncPending = Set.empty
    83→      AppVersion = None
    84→      EditorName = "VS Code"
    85→      EyeDirection = (0.0, 0.0)
    86→      FocusedElement = None
    87→      CreateModal = CreateWorktreeModal.Closed
    88→      DeletedBranches = Set.empty
    89→      DeployBranch = None
    90→      SystemMetrics = None },
    91→    Cmd.batch [ fetchWorktrees (); fetchSyncStatus () ]
    92→
    93→let rng = System.Random()
    94→
    95→let randomEyeDirection () =
    96→    let dx = rng.NextDouble() * 3.0 - 1.5
    97→    let dy = rng.NextDouble() * 2.0 - 1.0
    98→    (dx, dy)
    99→
   100→let filterDeletedBranches (deleted: Set<string>) (repos: RepoModel list) =
   101→    if Set.isEmpty deleted then repos
   102→    else
   103→        repos
   104→        |> List.map (fun r ->
   105→            { r with Worktrees = r.Worktrees |> List.filter (fun wt -> not (Set.contains wt.Branch deleted)) })
   106→
   107→let findWorktree (scopedKey: string) (model: Model) =
   108→    let parts = scopedKey.Split('/', 2)
   109→    if parts.Length < 2 then None
   110→    else
   111→        let repoId, branch = parts[0], parts[1]
   112→        model.Repos
   113→        |> List.tryFind (fun r -> RepoId.value r.RepoId = repoId)
   114→        |> Option.bind (fun r -> r.Worktrees |> List.tryFind (fun wt -> wt.Branch = branch))
   115→
   116→let terminalAction (wt: WorktreeStatus) =
   117→    if wt.HasActiveSession then FocusSession wt.Path else OpenTerminal wt.Path
   118→
   119→let keyBinding (focused: FocusTarget) (key: string) (model: Model) : Msg option =
   120→    match focused, key with
   121→    | Card scopedKey, "Enter" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map terminalAction
   122→    | Card scopedKey, "s" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map (fun wt -> StartSync (wt.Branch, scopedKey))
   123→    | Card scopedKey, "+" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.bind (fun wt -> if wt.HasActiveSession then Some (OpenNewTab wt.Path) else None)
   124→    | Card scopedKey, "e" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map (fun wt -> OpenEditor wt.Path)
   125→    | Card scopedKey, "a" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map (fun wt -> ArchiveMsg (ArchiveViews.Archive (BranchName.create wt.Branch)))
   126→    | Card scopedKey, "Delete" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map (fun wt -> ConfirmDeleteWorktree wt.Branch)
   127→    | RepoHeader repoId, "Enter" -> Some (ToggleCollapse repoId)
   128→    | RepoHeader repoId, "+" -> Some (ModalMsg (CreateWorktreeModal.OpenCreateWorktree repoId))
   129→    | _ -> None
   130→
   131→let update msg model =
   132→    match msg with
   133→    | DataLoaded response ->
   134→        match model.AppVersion with
   135→        | Some v when v <> response.AppVersion ->
   136→            model, Cmd.ofEffect (fun _ -> Dom.window.location.reload ())
   137→        | _ ->
   138→            let existingCollapse =
   139→                model.Repos
   140→                |> List.map (fun r -> r.RepoId, r.IsCollapsed)
   141→                |> Map.ofList
   142→            let serverBranches =
   143→                response.Repos
   144→                |> List.collect (fun r -> r.Worktrees |> List.map (fun wt -> wt.Branch))

FocusedElement: FocusTarget option
      CreateModal: CreateWorktreeModal.ModalState
      DeletedBranches: Set<string>
      DeployBranch: string option
      SystemMetrics: SystemMetrics option }

type Msg =
    | DataLoaded of DashboardResponse
    | DataFailed of exn
    | ToggleSort
    | ToggleCompact
    | ToggleCollapse of repoId: RepoId
    | Tick
    | OpenTerminal of WorktreePath
    | OpenEditor of WorktreePath
    | StartSync of branch: string * scopedKey: string
    | SyncStarted of key: string * Result<unit, string>
    | SyncStatusUpdate of Map<string, CardEvent list>
    | CancelSync of string
    | SyncTick
    | ConfirmDeleteWorktree of branch: string
    | DeleteWorktree of string
    | DeleteCompleted of Result<unit, string>
    | FocusSession of path: WorktreePath
    | OpenNewTab of path: WorktreePath
    | SessionResult of Result<unit, string>
    | KeyPressed of key: string * hasModifier: bool
    | SetFocus of FocusTarget option
    | ArchiveMsg of ArchiveViews.Msg
    | ModalMsg of CreateWorktreeModal.Msg

let worktreeApi =
    Remoting.createApi ()
    |> Remoting.buildProxy<IWorktreeApi>

let fetchWorktrees () =
    Cmd.OfAsync.either worktreeApi.getWorktrees () DataLoaded DataFailed

let fetchSyncStatus () =
    Cmd.OfAsync.perform worktreeApi.getSyncStatus () SyncStatusUpdate

let hasSyncRunning (events: Map<string, CardEvent list>) =
    events
    |> Map.exists (fun _ evts ->
        evts
        |> List.exists (fun e ->
            e.Status = Some StepStatus.Running))

let init () =
    { Repos = []
      IsLoading = true
      HasError = false
      SortMode = ByActivity
      IsCompact = false
      SchedulerEvents = []
      LatestByCategory = Map.empty
      BranchEvents = Map.empty
      SyncPending = Set.empty
      AppVersion = None
      EditorName = "VS Code"
      EyeDirection = (0.0, 0.0)
      FocusedElement = None
      CreateModal = CreateWorktreeModal.Closed
      DeletedBranches = Set.empty
      DeployBranch = None
      SystemMetrics = None },
    Cmd.batch [ fetchWorktrees (); fetchSyncStatus () ]

let rng = System.Random()

let randomEyeDirection () =
    let dx = rng.NextDouble() * 3.0 - 1.5
    let dy = rng.NextDouble() * 2.0 - 1.0
    (dx, dy)

let filterDeletedBranches (deleted: Set<string>) (repos: RepoModel list) =
    if Set.isEmpty deleted then repos
    else
        repos
        |> List.map (fun r ->
            { r with Worktrees = r.Worktrees |> List.filter (fun wt -> not (Set.contains wt.Branch deleted)) })

let findWorktree (scopedKey: string) (model: Model) =
    let parts = scopedKey.Split('/', 2)
    if parts.Length < 2 then None
    else
        let repoId, branch = parts[0], parts[1]
        model.Repos
        |> List.tryFind (fun r -> RepoId.value r.RepoId = repoId)
        |> Option.bind (fun r -> r.Worktrees |> List.tryFind (fun wt -> wt.Branch = branch))

let terminalAction (wt: WorktreeStatus) =
    if wt.HasActiveSession then FocusSession wt.Path else OpenTerminal wt.Path

let keyBinding (focused: FocusTarget) (key: string) (model: Model) : Msg option =
    match focused, key with
    | Card scopedKey, "Enter" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map terminalAction
    | Card scopedKey, "s" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map (fun wt -> StartSync (wt.Branch, scopedKey))
    | Card scopedKey, "+" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.bind (fun wt -> if wt.HasActiveSession then Some (OpenNewTab wt.Path) else None)
    | Card scopedKey, "e" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map (fun wt -> OpenEditor wt.Path)
    | Card scopedKey, "a" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map (fun wt -> ArchiveMsg (ArchiveViews.Archive (BranchName.create wt.Branch)))
    | Card scopedKey, "Delete" -> findWorktree scopedKey model |> Option.map (fun wt -> ConfirmDeleteWorktree wt.Branch)
    | RepoHeader repoId, "Enter" -> Some (ToggleCollapse repoId)
    | RepoHeader repoId, "+" -> Some (ModalMsg (CreateWorktreeModal.OpenCreateWorktree repoId))
    | _ -> None

let update msg model =
    match msg with
    | DataLoaded response ->
        match model.AppVersion with
        | Some v when v <> response.AppVersion ->
            model, Cmd.ofEffect (fun _ -> Dom.window.location.reload ())
        | _ ->
            let existingCollapse =
                model.Repos
                |> List.map (fun r -> r.RepoId, r.IsCollapsed)
                |> Map.ofList
            let serverBranches =
                response.Repos
                |> List.collect (fun r -> r.Worktrees |> List.map (fun wt -> wt.Branch))

|> List.tryFind (fun r -> r.RepoId = repoId)

            |> Option.defaultValue false
        let updatedModel =
            { model with
                Repos = model.Repos |> List.map (fun r ->
                    if r.RepoId = repoId then { r with IsCollapsed = not r.IsCollapsed }
                    else r) }
        let focusAdjusted =

    | OpenTerminal path ->
        model, Cmd.OfAsync.attempt worktreeApi.openTerminal path (fun _ -> Tick)
    | OpenEditor path ->
        model, Cmd.OfAsync.attempt worktreeApi.openEditor path (fun _ -> Tick)

    | Tick ->
        model, Cmd.batch [ fetchWorktrees (); fetchSyncStatus () ]

    | StartSync (branch, key) ->
        let syntheticEvent =
            { Source = "Sync"
              Message = "Sync starting"
              Timestamp = System.DateTimeOffset.Now
              Status = Some StepStatus.Running
              Duration = None }
        let updatedEvents =
            model.BranchEvents
            |> Map.add key [ syntheticEvent ]
        { model with
            SyncPending = model.SyncPending |> Set.add key
            BranchEvents = updatedEvents },
        Cmd.OfAsync.perform worktreeApi.startSync branch (fun r -> SyncStarted (key, r))

    | SyncStarted (key, Ok _) ->
        { model with SyncPending = model.SyncPending |> Set.remove key }, fetchSyncStatus ()

    | SyncStarted (key, Error _) ->
        { model with
            SyncPending = model.SyncPending |> Set.remove key
            BranchEvents = model.BranchEvents |> Map.remove key },
        Cmd.none

    | SyncStatusUpdate events ->
        { model with BranchEvents = events }, Cmd.none

    | CancelSync branch ->
        model, Cmd.OfAsync.attempt worktreeApi.cancelSync branch (fun _ -> Tick)

    | SyncTick ->
        model, fetchSyncStatus ()

    | ConfirmDeleteWorktree branch ->
        model, Cmd.ofEffect (fun dispatch ->
            if Dom.window.confirm $"Remove worktree {branch}? This will delete the worktree folder and local branch." then
                dispatch (DeleteWorktree branch))

    | DeleteWorktree branch ->
        let updatedRepos =
            model.Repos
            |> List.map (fun r ->
                { r with Worktrees = r.Worktrees |> List.filter (fun wt -> wt.Branch <> branch) })
        let updatedModel =
            { model with
                Repos = updatedRepos
                DeletedBranches = model.DeletedBranches |> Set.add branch }
        { updatedModel with FocusedElement = adjustFocusForVisibility updatedModel.Repos updatedModel.FocusedElement },
        Cmd.OfAsync.perform worktreeApi.deleteWorktree branch DeleteCompleted

    | DeleteCompleted (Ok _) ->
        model, fetchWorktrees ()

    | DeleteCompleted (Error _) ->
        { model with DeletedBranches = Set.empty }, fetchWorktrees ()

    | FocusSession path ->
        model, Cmd.OfAsync.perform worktreeApi.focusSession path SessionResult

    | OpenNewTab path ->
        model, Cmd.OfAsync.perform worktreeApi.openNewTab path SessionResult

    | SessionResult _ ->
        model, fetchWorktrees ()

    | SetFocus target ->
        { model with FocusedElement = target }, Cmd.none

    | ArchiveMsg archiveMsg ->
        let result, archiveCmd = ArchiveViews.update (lazy worktreeApi) archiveMsg
        let refreshCmd = if result.RefreshWorktrees then fetchWorktrees () else Cmd.none
        model, Cmd.batch [ Cmd.map ArchiveMsg archiveCmd; refreshCmd ]

    | ModalMsg modalMsg ->
        let result, modalCmd = CreateWorktreeModal.update (lazy worktreeApi) modalMsg model.CreateModal
        let focus = result.RestoredFocus |> Option.orElse model.FocusedElement
        let refreshCmd = if result.RefreshWorktrees then fetchWorktrees () else Cmd.none
        { model with CreateModal = result.Modal; FocusedElement = focus },
        Cmd.batch [ Cmd.map ModalMsg modalCmd; refreshCmd ]

    | KeyPressed (key, hasModifier) ->
        let scrollToFocus oldFocus newFocus =
            let useCenter = isLargeJump model.Repos oldFocus newFocus
            Cmd.ofEffect (fun _ -> scrollFocusedIntoView useCenter newFocus)
        if CreateWorktreeModal.isOpen model.CreateModal then
            match key with
            | "Escape" ->
                let restoredFocus =
                    CreateWorktreeModal.repoId model.CreateModal
                    |> Option.map RepoHeader
                    |> Option.orElse model.FocusedElement
                { model with CreateModal = CreateWorktreeModal.Closed; FocusedElement = restoredFocus },
                Cmd.none
            | _ -> model, Cmd.none
        else
        match key with
        | "ArrowDown" | "ArrowUp" | "ArrowLeft" | "ArrowRight" ->
            let cols = getColumnCount ()

file.content:
namespace Shared

open System

type RepoId = RepoId of string

module RepoId =
    let create (path: string) = RepoId path
    let value (RepoId id) = id

type WorktreePath = WorktreePath of string

module WorktreePath =
    let create (path: string) = WorktreePath path
    let value (WorktreePath p) = p

type BranchName = BranchName of string

module BranchName =
    let create (name: string) = BranchName name
    let value (BranchName b) = b

type BeadsSummary =
    { Open: int
      InProgress: int
      Closed: int }

module BeadsSummary =
    let zero = { Open = 0; InProgress = 0; Closed = 0 }

type CodingToolStatus =
    | Working
    | WaitingForUser
    | Done
    | Idle

type CodingToolProvider =
    | Claude
    | Copilot

type BuildStatus =
    | Building
    | Succeeded
    | Failed
    | PartiallySucceeded
    | Canceled

type CommentSummary =
    | WithResolution of unresolved: int * total: int
    | CountOnly of total: int

type BuildFailure =
    { StepName: string
      Log: string }

type BuildInfo =
    { Name: string
      Status: BuildStatus
      Url: string option
      Failure: BuildFailure option }

type PrStatus =
    | NoPr
    | HasPr of PrInfo

and PrInfo =
    { Id: int
      Title: string
      Url: string
      IsDraft: bool
      Comments: CommentSummary
      Builds: BuildInfo list
      IsMerged: bool }

type WorkMetrics =
    { CommitCount: int
      LinesAdded: int
      LinesRemoved: int }

type LaunchRequest =
    { Path: WorktreePath
      Prompt: string }

type CreateWorktreeRequest =
    { RepoId: string
      BranchName: BranchName
      BaseBranch: BranchName }

type WorktreeStatus =
    { Path: WorktreePath
      Branch: string
      LastCommitMessage: string
      LastCommitTime: DateTimeOffset
      Beads: BeadsSummary
      CodingTool: CodingToolStatus
      CodingToolProvider: CodingToolProvider option
      LastUserMessage: (string * DateTimeOffset) option
      Pr: PrStatus
      MainBehindCount: int
      IsDirty: bool
      WorkMetrics: WorkMetrics option
      HasActiveSession: bool
      IsArchived: bool }

[<RequireQualifiedAccess>]
type StepStatus =
    | Pending
    | Running
    | Succeeded
    | Failed of message: string
    | Cancelled

type CardEvent =
    { Source: string
      Message: string
      Timestamp: DateTimeOffset
      Status: StepStatus option
      Duration: TimeSpan option }

type RepoWorktrees =
    { RepoId: RepoId
      RootFolderName: string
      Worktrees: WorktreeStatus list
      IsReady: bool }

type SystemMetrics =
    { CpuPercent: float
      MemoryUsedMb: int
      MemoryTotalMb: int }

type DashboardResponse =
    { Repos: RepoWorktrees list
      SchedulerEvents: CardEvent list
      LatestByCategory: Map<string, CardEvent>
      AppVersion: string
      DeployBranch: string option
      SystemMetrics: SystemMetrics option
      EditorName: string }

type IWorktreeApi =
    { getWorktrees: unit -> Async<DashboardResponse>
      openTerminal: WorktreePath -> Async<unit>
      openEditor: WorktreePath -> Async<unit>
      startSync: string -> Async<Result<unit, string>>
      cancelSync: string -> Async<unit>
      getSyncStatus: unit -> Async<Map<string, CardEvent list>>
      deleteWorktree: string -> Async<Result<unit, string>>
      launchSession: LaunchRequest -> Async<Result<unit, string>>
      focusSession: WorktreePath -> Async<Result<unit, string>>

let private openEditor (validatePath: string -> Async<bool>) (wtPath: WorktreePath) =
    let path = WorktreePath.value wtPath
    async {
        let! isValid = validatePath path

        if not isValid then
            Log.log "API" $"openEditor: rejected unknown path '{path}'"
        else
            let editor, _ = getEditorConfig ()
            Log.log "API" $"openEditor: opening '{editor}' for '{path}'"

            try
                let psi =
                    System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo(
                        "cmd.exe",
                        $"/c {editor} \"{path}\"",
                        UseShellExecute = false,
                        CreateNoWindow = true
                    )

                System.Diagnostics.Process.Start(psi) |> ignore
            with ex ->
                Log.log "API" $"openEditor: failed for '{path}': {ex.Message}"
    }

let private openTerminal
    (validatePath: string -> Async<bool>)
    (sessionAgent: SessionManager.SessionAgent)
    (wtPath: WorktreePath)
    =
    let path = WorktreePath.value wtPath
    async {
        let! isValid = validatePath path

        if not isValid then
            Log.log "API" $"openTerminal: rejected unknown path '{path}'"
        else
            Log.log "API" $"openTerminal: launching terminal for '{path}'"
            let! result = SessionManager.spawnTerminal sessionAgent wtPath

            match result with
            | Ok () -> ()
            | Error msg -> Log.log "API" $"openTerminal: failed for '{path}': {msg}"
    }

let private deleteWorktree
    (agent: MailboxProcessor<RefreshScheduler.StateMsg>)
    (rootPaths: Map<RepoId, string>)
    (branch: string)
    =
    async {
        let! state = agent.PostAndAsyncReply(RefreshScheduler.StateMsg.GetState)
        let worktrees = allWorktrees state

        let worktree =
            worktrees
            |> List.tryFind (fun wt -> wt.Branch = Some branch)

        match worktree with
        | None -> return Error $"No worktree found for branch '{branch}'"
        | Some wt ->
            let repoId = findRepoForPath state wt.Path

            let repoRoot =
                repoId
                |> Option.bind (fun rid -> rootPaths |> Map.tryFind rid)

            match repoId, repoRoot with
            | Some rid, Some root ->
                agent.Post(RefreshScheduler.StateMsg.RemoveWorktree(rid, wt.Path))
                let! result = GitWorktree.removeWorktree root wt.Path branch
                return result
            | _ ->
                return Error $"Could not identify repo root for worktree at '{wt.Path}'"
    }

let private updateArchivedBranches
    (agent: MailboxProcessor<RefreshScheduler.StateMsg>)

questions.question: For sending stdin to a coding tool running in Windows Terminal — wt.exe is a launcher that exits immediately, so there's no stdin pipe to the running shell. Two approaches: (A) launch the coding tool with the prompt baked into the command line (like `claude "/pr <url>"`) which starts a fresh interactive session with that initial prompt, or (B) try to use Windows APIs (SendInput/WM_CHAR) to type text into the focused terminal window. Which approach?

options.label: Command-line prompt (Recommended)
options.description: Launch `claude '<prompt>'` or `copilot -p '<prompt>'` in a new tab via wt.exe. The tool starts interactive with that prompt. Simple, reliable, already proven by spawnAndResolve. No stdin pipe needed.

options.label: SendInput/keyboard simulation
options.description: Focus the terminal window, then use Win32 SendInput to simulate typing. Complex, fragile (clipboard issues, focus races), but could target an already-running session.

questions.question: For the 'no session exists' case — when the user clicks a contextual action button and there's no tracked terminal window, should we spawn a new window, or open a new tab in the most recently focused WT window?

options.label: Spawn new window (Recommended)
options.description: Consistent with current model (one window per worktree). Spawns `wt.exe --window new`, tracks HWND. Action button = spawn + launch tool in one step.

options.label: Open tab in existing WT
options.description: Uses `wt.exe -w 0 new-tab` to open in the focused window. Saves screen space but breaks the one-window-per-worktree model and HWND tracking.

questions.question: For the 'session already exists' case — when there IS a tracked window and user clicks a contextual action, should we open a new tab in that window (running the coding tool there), or kill the existing session and respawn?

options.label: New tab in tracked window (Recommended)
options.description: Opens a new tab via `wt.exe -w 0 new-tab` in the focused tracked window. Non-destructive — existing work in other tabs is preserved.

options.label: Kill and respawn window
options.description: Kills the existing window and spawns a fresh one with the coding tool. Destructive — any running sessions in that window are lost.

answers.For sending stdin to a coding tool runni: If command line works it's perfect, but we cannot use "-p" - because that doesn't open interactive session. A critical goal is that the session started is interactive and stays open for user to check it later.

content.text: Important clarification — the session must be interactive, so we can't use `-p` which runs non-interactively. Let me investigate how to start Claude Code interactively with a pre-loaded prompt. The current `spawnAndResolve` uses `claude '{prompt}'` (not `-p`) which does start interactively. input.prompt:
I need to understand how `claude` CLI (Claude Code) can be started interactively with an initial prompt/message. The key constraint is:
- `-p` flag runs non-interactively (exits after response) — we do NOT want this
- We need the session to stay open for the user to interact with

Research the current SessionManager.fs approach at Q:\code\tm-actions\src\Server\SessionManager.fs line 71-75:
```fsharp
let private spawnAndResolve (worktreePath: string) (prompt: string) =
    let nativePath = worktreePath.Replace('/', Path.DirectorySeparatorChar)
    let escapedPrompt = prompt.Replace("'", "''")
    let encoded = encodeCommand $"Set-Location '{nativePath}'; claude '{escapedPrompt}'"
    spawnWtAndResolve $"--window new -- pwsh -NoExit -EncodedCommand {encoded}" "session"
```

This runs `claude '<prompt>'` which should start Claude Code with that initial message in interactive mode.

Also look at the SyncEngine.fs line 245-250 conflict resolution approach which uses `-p` for non-interactive:
```fsharp
"claude", """-p "/conflict" --dangerously-skip-permissions"""
```

Questions to investigate:
1. Does `claude '<initial-message>'` start interactive mode with that message sent first? This is the current approach in SessionManager.
2. For Copilot CLI, what's the equivalent to start interactively with a message?
3. Could we use `echo '<prompt>' | claude` to pipe stdin? But with -NoExit, does the terminal stay interactive?
4. Is there a `--resume` or `--continue` flag for Claude CLI that might be relevant?

Search the web for "claude code CLI interactive mode initial message" and "claude code CLI arguments" to find the latest documentation.

content.text:
I now have all the information needed. Here is the comprehensive analysis:

---

## Research Results: Claude CLI & Copilot CLI Interactive Mode with Initial Prompt

### Question 1: Does `claude '<initial-message>'` start interactive mode with that message sent first?

**Yes, this is correct and well-documented.** The official Claude Code CLI reference at `https://code.claude.com/docs/en/cli-reference` explicitly documents this pattern:

| Command | Description | Example |
|---------|-------------|---------|
| `claude` | Start interactive session | `claude` |
| `claude "query"` | Start interactive session with initial prompt | `claude "explain this project"` |
| `claude -p "query"` | Query via SDK, then exit | `claude -p "explain this function"` |

The key distinction:
- **`claude "query"`** (positional argument, no `-p` flag) -- Starts the **interactive REPL** with that prompt sent as the first message. The session stays open for the user to continue interacting.
- **`claude -p "query"`** (with `-p` flag) -- **Non-interactive print mode**. Executes the query and exits.

**The current approach in `SessionManager.fs` (line 74) is correct:**
```fsharp
let encoded = encodeCommand $"Set-Location '{nativePath}'; claude '{escapedPrompt}'"
```

This runs `claude '<prompt>'` which starts an interactive session with `<prompt>` as the initial message. Combined with `pwsh -NoExit`, this ensures the terminal stays open even if `claude` exits (though `claude` in interactive mode would remain open on its own -- the `-NoExit` is a safety net).

### Question 2: For Copilot CLI, what's the equivalent to start interactively with a message?

**Copilot CLI has a dedicated `-i` / `--interactive` flag for this exact use case.** From the GitHub Copilot CLI command reference (`https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/reference/cli-command-reference`):

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `-i PROMPT`, `--interactive PROMPT` | Start an interactive session and automatically execute this prompt |
| `-p PROMPT`, `--prompt PROMPT` | Execute a prompt programmatically (exits after completion) |

So the Copilot equivalent of `claude "initial message"` is:

```
copilot -i "initial message"
```

This starts an interactive session and auto-sends the prompt, but the session remains open for continued interaction.

The current SyncEngine.fs (line 249-250) correctly uses `-p` for non-interactive conflict resolution:
```fsharp
"copilot", """-p "Resolve all merge conflicts..." --allow-all --no-ask-user -s --autopilot"""
```

If you wanted to launch an interactive Copilot session with a prompt (for `SessionManager`), you would use:
```
copilot -i "your prompt here"
```

### Question 3: Could we use `echo '<prompt>' | claude` to pipe stdin?

**This is not recommended for interactive sessions.** The pipe approach (`echo '<prompt>' | claude -p`) is documented for non-interactive use only. When you pipe stdin, the CLI enters print mode behavior. With `-NoExit` on pwsh, the terminal window would stay open, but claude itself would have already consumed the piped stdin and exited.

The positional argument approach (`claude "query"`) is cleaner and purpose-built for this scenario.

### Question 4: Is there a `--resume` or `--continue` flag for Claude CLI?

**Yes, both exist:**

| Flag | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| `--continue`, `-c` | Continue most recent conversation in current directory | `claude -c` |
| `--resume`, `-r` | Resume a specific session by ID or name, or show an interactive picker | `claude --resume auth-refactor` |
| `--fork-session` | When resuming, create a new session ID instead of reusing the original (use with `--resume` or `--continue`) | `claude --resume abc123 --fork-session` |
| `--session-id` | Use a specific session ID (must be valid UUID) | `claude --session-id "550e..."` |

These can also be combined with the initial prompt:
```
claude -r "session-id" "continue working on this feature"
```

For Copilot CLI, the equivalent flags are:
- `--continue` -- Resume the most recent session
- `--resume [SESSION-ID]` -- Resume a previous interactive session by choosing from a list (optionally specify a session ID)

### Summary Table

| Use Case | Claude Code CLI | GitHub Copilot CLI |
|----------|----------------|--------------------|
| Interactive, no prompt | `claude` | `copilot` |
| Interactive + initial prompt | `claude "prompt"` | `copilot -i "prompt"` |
| Non-interactive (exit after) | `claude -p "prompt"` | `copilot -p "prompt"` |
| Continue last session | `claude -c` | `copilot --continue` |
| Resume by ID | `claude -r "id"` | `copilot --resume "id"` |

### Current Code Assessment

The `SessionManager.fs` approach at line 71-75 is **correct** for launching interactive Claude sessions with an initial prompt:

```fsharp
let private spawnAndResolve (worktreePath: string) (prompt: string) =
    let nativePath = worktreePath.Replace('/', Path.DirectorySeparatorChar)
    let escapedPrompt = prompt.Replace("'", "''")
    let encoded = encodeCommand $"Set-Location '{nativePath}'; claude '{escapedPrompt}'"
    spawnWtAndResolve $"--window new -- pwsh -NoExit -EncodedCommand {encoded}" "session"
```

If you wanted to support launching Copilot CLI sessions interactively with a prompt, the equivalent command string would be:
```
copilot -i '{escapedPrompt}'
```

The `SyncEngine.fs` approach at line 245-250 is also **correct** for non-interactive conflict resolution, using `-p` for both Claude and Copilot since those should run to completion and exit.

If you wanted to support launching Copilot CLI sessions interactively with a prompt, the equivalent command string would be:
```
copilot -i '{escapedPrompt}'
```

The `SyncEngine.fs` approach at line 245-250 is also **correct** for non-interactive conflict resolution, using `-p` for both Claude and Copilot since those should run to completion and exit.

Good point — when the coding tool is `Working`, the action buttons should be disabled, just like the sync button already is. Let me incorporate that into the analysis.

Let me check exactly how the sync button disabling works for reference.

let syncButton dispatch (wt: WorktreeStatus) (branchEvents: CardEvent list) (isPending: bool) (scopedKey: string) =
    if isPending then
        Html.button [
            prop.className "sync-starting-btn"
            prop.disabled true
            yield! noFocusProps
            prop.text "Sync starting"
        ]
    else
        let syncing = isBranchSyncing branchEvents
        let claudeBlocked = wt.CodingTool = Working || wt.CodingTool = WaitingForUser
        let disabled = syncing || claudeBlocked
        if syncing then
            Html.button [
                prop.className "sync-cancel-btn"
                yield! noFocusProps
                prop.onClick (fun e -> e.stopPropagation(); dispatch (CancelSync wt.Branch))
                prop.text "Cancel"
            ]
        else
            Html.button [
                prop.className (if disabled then "sync-btn disabled" else "sync-btn")
                prop.disabled disabled
                yield! noFocusProps
                prop.onClick (fun e -> e.stopPropagation(); dispatch (StartSync (wt.Branch, scopedKey)))
                prop.title (if claudeBlocked then $"{providerDisplayName wt.CodingToolProvider} is active" else "Sync with main (S)")
                prop.text "Sync"
            ]

let mainBehindWithSync dispatch (wt: WorktreeStatus) (branchEvents: CardEvent list) (isPending: bool) (scopedKey: string) =
    Html.div [
        prop.className "main-behind-row"
        prop.children [
            mainBehindIndicator wt.MainBehindCount
            if wt.MainBehindCount > 0 then
                if wt.IsDirty then
                    Html.span [
                        prop.className "dirty-warning"
                        prop.text "uncommitted changes"
                    ]
                else syncButton dispatch wt branchEvents isPending scopedKey
            Html.span [
                prop.className "git-commit-msg"
                prop.children [
                    Html.text wt.LastCommitMessage
                    Html.span [ prop.className "commit-time"; prop.text (relativeTime System.DateTimeOffset.Now wt.LastCommitTime) ]
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]

input.content:
# Contextual Action Buttons Investigation

## Problem Statement

Add contextual action buttons to worktree cards that launch a coding tool (Claude/Copilot) in interactive mode with a pre-loaded prompt, targeting specific workflows:
- **Unresolved PR comments**: `/pr <PR-URL>`
- **Failing build**: `/fix-build <build-URL>`
- **No PR exists**: "Commit, push and make a PR"

Buttons should use existing session management (spawn window if none, open new tab if tracked window exists) and be disabled when the coding tool is already `Working` or `WaitingForUser`.

## Symptoms

Not a bug — this is a feature request. Current cards display PR/build status as read-only badges with no way to take action from the dashboard.

## Evidence Gathered

### Existing Infrastructure (Ready to Use)

The codebase already has nearly all the plumbing needed:

1. **`launchSession` API** (`src/Shared/Types.fs:148`, `src/Server/WorktreeApi.fs:412-414`)
   - Takes `LaunchRequest = { Path: WorktreePath; Prompt: string }`
   - Spawns a new Windows Terminal window, runs `claude '<prompt>'` interactively
   - Tracks HWND for later focus/kill
   - Already wired end-to-end but **not yet exposed as a card button** (spec note at `docs/spec/native-session-management.md:28-29`: "Not exposed as a card button yet — future contextual actions will use this")

2. **`openNewTab` API** (`src/Server/SessionManager.fs:82-104`)
   - Opens a new tab in a tracked window via `wt.exe -w 0 new-tab`
   - Currently only opens a plain shell (no command)
   - **Needs modification**: accept an optional command to run in the new tab

3. **Session tracking** (`src/Server/SessionManager.fs`)
   - `HasActiveSession: bool` on `WorktreeStatus` — already tracks whether a window exists
   - HWND-based tracking, persistence, validation all working

4. **PR/Build data with URLs** (`src/Shared/Types.fs:56-73`)
   - `PrInfo.Url: string` — full PR URL available
   - `BuildInfo.Url: string option` — build/workflow run URL available
   - `CommentSummary.WithResolution(unresolved, total)` — unresolved comment count (AzDo)
   - `CommentSummary.CountOnly(total)` — comment count (GitHub)

5. **Coding tool provider detection** (`src/Server/CodingToolStatus.fs`)
   - `CodingToolProvider` (Claude|Copilot) already detected per worktree
   - Provider determines CLI command format

6. **Disabled-when-active pattern** (`src/Client/App.fs:455-456`)
   - Sync button already disables when `wt.CodingTool = Working || wt.CodingTool = WaitingForUser`
   - Same pattern reusable for new action buttons

### Affected Components

- `src/Shared/Types.fs` — New `LaunchInTabRequest` type or extend `LaunchRequest`
- `src/Server/SessionManager.fs:82-104` — `openNewTabInWindow` needs to accept optional command
- `src/Server/WorktreeApi.fs` — New API endpoint or extend `openNewTab`
- `src/Client/App.fs:780-825` — PR row needs action buttons
- `src/Client/App.fs:671-716` — Build badges need action buttons
- `src/Client/App.fs:31-54` — New `Msg` variants for contextual actions
- `src/Client/App.fs:131-280` — Update handler for new messages
- `src/Client/index.html` — CSS for new button styles

### Related Code Areas

- `src/Server/SyncEngine.fs:245-250` — Conflict resolution command pattern (non-interactive, uses `-p`)
- `src/Server/SessionManager.fs:71-75` — `spawnAndResolve` (interactive, uses `claude '<prompt>'`)
- `src/Client/App.fs:445-472` — Sync button disabled state (reference pattern)

## Root Cause Analysis

This is new functionality. The infrastructure was intentionally designed for this — the spec explicitly notes `launchSession` as "for future contextual actions" (`docs/spec/native-session-management.md:29`).

### Key Design Decisions

**Interactive mode via command-line argument (not `-p`):**
- Claude: `claude "<prompt>"` — starts interactive REPL with initial prompt
- Copilot: `copilot -i "<prompt>"` — starts interactive session with initial prompt
- The `-p` flag on both tools runs non-interactively and exits — NOT what we want
- Session stays open for user to review and continue interacting

**Disable when coding tool is active:**
- When `CodingTool = Working` or `WaitingForUser`, action buttons are disabled
- Matches existing sync button behavior (`App.fs:455`)
- Tooltip shows "{provider} is active" when disabled

**Session handling for action buttons:**
- **No tracked window**: Spawn new window with coding tool + prompt (use existing `launchSession`)
- **Tracked window exists**: Open new tab in that window with coding tool + prompt (extend `openNewTab`)

## Proposed Solution

### Server Changes

**1. Extend `openNewTab` to accept an optional command**

Modify `SessionManager.openNewTabInWindow` to accept an optional command parameter. When provided, the new tab runs `pwsh -NoExit -EncodedCommand {cmd}` instead of just opening a shell.

New API endpoint or extend existing:
```
launchInTab: LaunchRequest -> Async<Result<unit, string>>
```

Logic:
- If tracked session exists → open new tab with `claude "<prompt>"` (or `copilot -i "<prompt>"`)
- If no tracked session → spawn new window with `claude "<prompt>"` (existing `launchSession` behavior)

This could be a single smart endpoint that checks session state and picks the right path.

**2. Provider-aware command construction**

Shared function to build the interactive launch command:
```fsharp
let interactiveCommand (provider: CodingToolProvider option) (prompt: string) =
    match provider |> Option.defaultValue Claude with
    | Claude -> $"claude '{escaped}'"
    | Copilot -> $"copilot -i '{escaped}'"
```

### Client Changes

**3. New Msg variants**

```fsharp
| LaunchAction of path: WorktreePath * prompt: string
| LaunchActionResult of Result<unit, string>
```

Single message type — the server decides whether to spawn window or open tab.

**4. Action buttons on PR row**

- **Unresolved PR comments** (AzDo: `unresolved > 0`, GitHub: `total > 0`): Button next to thread badge → launches `/pr <PR-URL>`
- **Failing build**: Button next to failed build badge → launches `/fix-build <build-URL>`
- **No PR**: Button in card (where PR row would be) → launches commit/push/PR prompt

**5. Disabled state**

All action buttons disabled when `wt.CodingTool = Working || wt.CodingTool = WaitingForUser`, using the same pattern as the sync button.

### Prompt Templates

| Condition | Prompt |
|-----------|--------|
| Unresolved PR comments | `/pr <pr-url>` |
| Failing build | `/fix-build <build-url>` |
| No PR exists | Commit, push and create a PR (exact prompt TBD) |

**Pros:**
- Leverages existing infrastructure — `launchSession`, HWND tracking, provider detection all ready
- Consistent UX — same disabled-when-active pattern as sync
- Interactive sessions let user review and continue
- One-window-per-worktree model preserved (new tab for additional actions)

**Cons:**
- Each action button opens a *new* tab/window with a fresh Claude/Copilot session (not reusing an existing session)
- GitHub doesn't provide resolved/unresolved comment distinction — button logic differs by provider
- Users must have `claude` or `copilot` CLI installed and on PATH

**Implementation Considerations:**
- The `openNewTabInWindow` function currently uses `wt.exe -w 0 new-tab` which targets the *focused* window. We need to ensure we focus the tracked HWND first (which it already does at line 87-88), then open the tab.
- Prompt escaping is critical — PR URLs contain special characters. The existing `escapedPrompt.Replace("'", "''")`  handles single quotes for PowerShell.
- For "No PR" action, the prompt needs to be a meaningful instruction like: `"Commit all changes, push to origin, and create a pull request for this branch"`

## Impact Assessment

- **Scope:** Server (SessionManager, WorktreeApi, Types), Client (App.fs, index.html)
- **Risk Level:** Low — extends existing infrastructure, no changes to core refresh loop or state management
- **Breaking Changes:** No — new API endpoints and UI elements only
- **Testing Requirements:**
  - Unit: prompt construction, provider-aware command building
  - E2E: button visibility based on PR/build state, disabled state when coding tool active
  - Manual: actual terminal spawn/tab with coding tool launch (Explicit tests)

## Verification Strategy

- E2E tests for button visibility: buttons appear only when conditions are met (unresolved comments, failed build, no PR)
- E2E tests for disabled state: buttons disabled when coding tool status is Working/WaitingForUser
- Manual spawn tests (Explicit): verify terminal opens with correct coding tool and prompt
- Verify existing session management still works (focus, kill, new tab)

## Next Steps

1. Use this investigation as input to `/bd-plan` for implementation planning
2. Key implementation order:
   - Extend server: `SessionManager` tab-with-command, new API endpoint
   - Add shared types: `LaunchInTabRequest` or extend `LaunchRequest`
   - Add client messages and update handler
   - Add action buttons to PR row and build badges
   - Add "No PR" action button
   - CSS for new button styles
   - E2E tests for button visibility and disabled states

content.text:
Created `.agents/contextual-actions-investigation.md` — investigation complete.

**Key findings:**

1. **Infrastructure is already built** — the spec explicitly designed `launchSession` for "future contextual actions" (`docs/spec/native-session-management.md:29`). The API, HWND tracking, and provider detection are all ready.

2. **Interactive launch works via positional argument** — `claude "<prompt>"` starts interactive mode (stays open), `copilot -i "<prompt>"` is the equivalent. The `-p` flag is only for non-interactive use.

3. **One gap to fill** — `openNewTabInWindow` currently only opens a plain shell. It needs to accept an optional command so new tabs can launch `claude "<prompt>"` or `copilot -i "<prompt>"`.

4. **Three action buttons needed:**
   - PR comments badge → launches `/pr <PR-URL>` 
   - Failed build badge → launches `/fix-build <build-URL>`
   - No PR card area → launches commit/push/PR prompt

5. **Disabled when active** — all action buttons disabled when `CodingTool = Working || WaitingForUser`, matching existing sync button pattern at `App.fs:455`.

6. **Smart endpoint** — single API call that checks whether a tracked window exists (open new tab) or not (spawn new window), so the client doesn't need to track this.

Use this investigation as input to `/bd-plan` for implementation planning.

Create a beads-based implementation plan from: $ARGUMENTS

**Argument Discovery:**
- If $ARGUMENTS is a file path that exists, read it as investigation/plan content
- Otherwise, treat $ARGUMENTS as the task description directly

Examples:
- `/bd-plan "Add user authentication with JWT and RBAC"`
- `/bd-plan .agents/data-layer-investigation.md`

<context_preservation>
Your job is planning, not investigation. Don't burn your own context reading source files. If you need to explore the codebase to understand existing patterns, architecture, or available libraries, delegate research to Task agents (subagent_type: "Explore" or "general-purpose"). Get answers back from agents and use them to inform the plan.
</context_preservation>

Follow the spec-management skill (skills/spec-management/SKILL.md) for canonical format and writing principles. At minimum, a new spec must have: Goals, Expected Behavior, Technical Approach. Add other sections (Decisions, Overview, Key Files, Related Specs) only when they carry information that isn't obvious from the code or context.
</spec_format>

<execution_model>
When tasks are executed via /bd-execute, they run ONE AT A TIME — never in parallel, even when dependencies would permit. Working in parallel on the same repo causes conflicts. Dependencies define ordering, not parallelization.
</execution_model>

<beads_reference>
- Status values: open, in_progress, blocked, closed (underscores, not hyphens)
- Dependency types: parent-child, blocks, related, discovered-from
- Issue types: epic, feature, task, bug, chore
</beads_reference>

**Steps:**

1. **Get working directory:**
   ```bash
   git rev-parse --show-toplevel
   ```

2. **Auto-initialize beads (if needed):**
   Check if `.beads/` directory exists in repo root. If not:
   ```bash
   bd init --stealth --quiet
   ```

3. **Read/analyze content:**
   - If file path: Read the investigation/plan file
   - If description: Use it directly
   - Extract: Goals, technical approach, key decisions, expected behavior

4. **Create or locate spec file:**
   - Generate spec filename from feature name: `docs/spec/[feature-name].md`
   - If file exists: Read it, use as authoritative context
   - If file doesn't exist: Create it (see spec_format above)

5. **Create beads feature:**
   ```bash
   bd create "[feature title]" -t feature -p 1 -d "Spec: docs/spec/[feature-name].md" --json
   ```

6. **Create child tasks:**
   For each discrete task:
   ```bash
   bd create "[task title]" -t task -p 2 -d "Spec: docs/spec/[feature-name].md - [brief task scope]" --json
   ```

   **Verification tasks** (E2E tests, benchmarks, integration validation, anything whose purpose is to *run something and prove it works*):
   ```bash
   bd create "[task title]" -t task -p 2 -l verify -d "Spec: docs/spec/[feature-name].md - [brief task scope]" --json
   ```
   The `verify` label routes these to a specialized reviewer that validates execution evidence rather than reviewing code.

   **Task sizing:** Each task should be completable within ~20-40K tokens (<<200K limit). Smaller tasks = better focus, less context pollution, easier recovery from issues.

7. **Add dependencies:**
   ```bash
   bd dep add [task-id] [feature-id] --type parent-child
   bd dep add [blocked-id] [blocker-id] --type blocks
   ```

8. **Plan verification strategy:**
   After creating implementation tasks, consider: how will we prove this feature satisfies its spec goals?

   Propose a verification approach to the user. Examples:
   - E2E tests with Playwright/Selenium that exercise the actual UI
   - Integration tests that call real APIs and check responses
   - Benchmark runs that measure performance against spec targets
   - Manual verification steps (if automation isn't practical)

   If no automated verification is feasible, say so — some features (CLI tools, infrastructure changes) may not lend themselves to automated verification. That's fine, but discuss it explicitly rather than skipping it silently.

   Create verification tasks with the `verify` label after the user agrees on the approach. These should depend on the implementation tasks they verify.

   **Wait for user response on verification before proceeding to step 9.**

9. **Add comprehensive review task (quality gate):**

   Always create a comprehensive-review task that runs after all implementation tasks but before any verification tasks. This is a holistic multi-model code review of the entire branch — catching cross-cutting issues (inconsistent patterns, architectural drift, duplication) that per-task reviews miss.

   ```bash
   bd create "Run comprehensive multi-model code review" -t task -p 2 \
     -d "Spec: docs/spec/[feature-name].md - Quality gate: multi-model code review.

   1. git fetch origin
   2. python ~/.claude/scripts/comprehensive-review.py branch  (20-min timeout)
   3. Read the ::SUMMARY::<path> line from output, then read the consolidated report at that path.
   4. For significant issues: create a fix task per issue (or closely related group) as a child of this feature.
      Each fix task MUST have a blocks dependency on every verification task (verify-labeled) in this feature.
   5. If no significant issues: note 'clean' in evidence.

   Evidence: path to consolidated report + fix task IDs created (or 'clean')." --json
   ```

   Add dependencies:
   ```bash
   bd dep add [review-task-id] [feature-id] --type parent-child
   bd dep add [review-task-id] [impl-task-id] --type blocks    # for EACH implementation task
   bd dep add [verify-task-id] [review-task-id] --type blocks  # for EACH verification task
   ```

   This creates the ordering: implementation → comprehensive review → (fix tasks if any) → verification.

   Fix tasks created during execution will block verification tasks, so verification only runs after code is in its best shape.

10. **Review plan via bd-plan-improver subagent:**

   After creating all tasks and dependencies, use the Task tool to spawn a `bd-plan-improver` agent. It reads everything from disk with fresh context — no memory of your planning decisions — so it catches blind spots.

   Use exactly this Task tool call (do not use /bd-autoimprove or /bd-improve — use the bd-plan-improver agent type):
   ```
   Task tool:
     subagent_type: "bd-plan-improver"
     description: "Review beads plan: [feature-id]"
     prompt: "[feature-id] | [spec-path]"
   ```

   The agent fixes mechanical issues (sizing, deps, clarity, spec sharpening) autonomously and returns two things: a list of improvements it applied, and questions about requirements/behavior it couldn't resolve on its own.

   **After subagent returns:**

   Present its output to the user:
   - Show the improvements that were applied
   - If there are questions, present them and wait for answers
   - After user answers, apply the answer-driven changes yourself (update spec/tasks based on what the user said), or spawn another small subagent if the changes are complex

<output_format>
Report to user:
- Spec file path (created or existing)
- Feature ID
- Number of tasks created
- Improvements applied by plan review (from bd-plan-improver)
- Questions (if any) — wait for answers before declaring done
- Next steps: /bd-execute
</output_format>

Don't implement anything — only create spec, beads structure, and improve the plan.
I'll start by reading the investigation file and setting up beads.

file.content:
# Contextual Action Buttons Investigation

## Problem Statement

Add contextual action buttons to worktree cards that launch a coding tool (Claude/Copilot) in interactive mode with a pre-loaded prompt, targeting specific workflows:
- **Unresolved PR comments**: `/pr <PR-URL>`
- **Failing build**: `/fix-build <build-URL>`
- **No PR exists**: "Commit, push and make a PR"

Buttons should use existing session management (spawn window if none, open new tab if tracked window exists) and be disabled when the coding tool is already `Working` or `WaitingForUser`.

## Symptoms

Not a bug — this is a feature request. Current cards display PR/build status as read-only badges with no way to take action from the dashboard.

## Evidence Gathered

### Existing Infrastructure (Ready to Use)

The codebase already has nearly all the plumbing needed:

1. **`launchSession` API** (`src/Shared/Types.fs:148`, `src/Server/WorktreeApi.fs:412-414`)
   - Takes `LaunchRequest = { Path: WorktreePath; Prompt: string }`
   - Spawns a new Windows Terminal window, runs `claude '<prompt>'` interactively
   - Tracks HWND for later focus/kill
   - Already wired end-to-end but **not yet exposed as a card button** (spec note at `docs/spec/native-session-management.md:28-29`: "Not exposed as a card button yet — future contextual actions will use this")

2. **`openNewTab` API** (`src/Server/SessionManager.fs:82-104`)
   - Opens a new tab in a tracked window via `wt.exe -w 0 new-tab`
   - Currently only opens a plain shell (no command)
   - **Needs modification**: accept an optional command to run in the new tab

3. **Session tracking** (`src/Server/SessionManager.fs`)
   - `HasActiveSession: bool` on `WorktreeStatus` — already tracks whether a window exists
   - HWND-based tracking, persistence, validation all working

4. **PR/Build data with URLs** (`src/Shared/Types.fs:56-73`)
   - `PrInfo.Url: string` — full PR URL available
   - `BuildInfo.Url: string option` — build/workflow run URL available
   - `CommentSummary.WithResolution(unresolved, total)` — unresolved comment count (AzDo)
   - `CommentSummary.CountOnly(total)` — comment count (GitHub)

5. **Coding tool provider detection** (`src/Server/CodingToolStatus.fs`)
   - `CodingToolProvider` (Claude|Copilot) already detected per worktree
   - Provider determines CLI command format

6. **Disabled-when-active pattern** (`src/Client/App.fs:455-456`)
   - Sync button already disables when `wt.CodingTool = Working || wt.CodingTool = WaitingForUser`
   - Same pattern reusable for new action buttons

### Affected Components

- `src/Shared/Types.fs` — New `LaunchInTabRequest` type or extend `LaunchRequest`
- `src/Server/SessionManager.fs:82-104` — `openNewTabInWindow` needs to accept optional command
- `src/Server/WorktreeApi.fs` — New API endpoint or extend `openNewTab`
- `src/Client/App.fs:780-825` — PR row needs action buttons
- `src/Client/App.fs:671-716` — Build badges need action buttons
- `src/Client/App.fs:31-54` — New `Msg` variants for contextual actions
- `src/Client/App.fs:131-280` — Update handler for new messages
- `src/Client/index.html` — CSS for new button styles

### Related Code Areas

- `src/Server/SyncEngine.fs:245-250` — Conflict resolution command pattern (non-interactive, uses `-p`)
- `src/Server/SessionManager.fs:71-75` — `spawnAndResolve` (interactive, uses `claude '<prompt>'`)
- `src/Client/App.fs:445-472` — Sync button disabled state (reference pattern)

## Root Cause Analysis

This is new functionality. The infrastructure was intentionally designed for this — the spec explicitly notes `launchSession` as "for future contextual actions" (`docs/spec/native-session-management.md:29`).

### Key Design Decisions

**Interactive mode via command-line argument (not `-p`):**
- Claude: `claude "<prompt>"` — starts interactive REPL with initial prompt
- Copilot: `copilot -i "<prompt>"` — starts interactive session with initial prompt
- The `-p` flag on both tools runs non-interactively and exits — NOT what we want
- Session stays open for user to review and continue interacting

**Disable when coding tool is active:**
- When `CodingTool = Working` or `WaitingForUser`, action buttons are disabled
- Matches existing sync button behavior (`App.fs:455`)
- Tooltip shows "{provider} is active" when disabled

**Session handling for action buttons:**
- **No tracked window**: Spawn new window with coding tool + prompt (use existing `launchSession`)
- **Tracked window exists**: Open new tab in that window with coding tool + prompt (extend `openNewTab`)

## Proposed Solution

### Server Changes

**1. Extend `openNewTab` to accept an optional command**

Modify `SessionManager.openNewTabInWindow` to accept an optional command parameter. When provided, the new tab runs `pwsh -NoExit -EncodedCommand {cmd}` instead of just opening a shell.

New API endpoint or extend existing:
```
launchInTab: LaunchRequest -> Async<Result<unit, string>>
```

Logic:
- If tracked session exists → open new tab with `claude "<prompt>"` (or `copilot -i "<prompt>"`)
- If no tracked session → spawn new window with `claude "<prompt>"` (existing `launchSession` behavior)

This could be a single smart endpoint that checks session state and picks the right path.

**2. Provider-aware command construction**

Shared function to build the interactive launch command:
```fsharp
let interactiveCommand (provider: CodingToolProvider option) (prompt: string) =
    match provider |> Option.defaultValue Claude with
    | Claude -> $"claude '{escaped}'"
    | Copilot -> $"copilot -i '{escaped}'"
```

### Client Changes

**3. New Msg variants**

```fsharp
| LaunchAction of path: WorktreePath * prompt: string
| LaunchActionResult of Result<unit, string>
```

Single message type — the server decides whether to spawn window or open tab.

**4. Action buttons on PR row**

- **Unresolved PR comments** (AzDo: `unresolved > 0`, GitHub: `total > 0`): Button next to thread badge → launches `/pr <PR-URL>`
- **Failing build**: Button next to failed build badge → launches `/fix-build <build-URL>`
- **No PR**: Button in card (where PR row would be) → launches commit/push/PR prompt

**5. Disabled state**

All action buttons disabled when `wt.CodingTool = Working || wt.CodingTool = WaitingForUser`, using the same pattern as the sync button.

### Prompt Templates

| Condition | Prompt |
|-----------|--------|
| Unresolved PR comments | `/pr <pr-url>` |
| Failing build | `/fix-build <build-url>` |
| No PR exists | Commit, push and create a PR (exact prompt TBD) |

**Pros:**
- Leverages existing infrastructure — `launchSession`, HWND tracking, provider detection all ready
- Consistent UX — same disabled-when-active pattern as sync
- Interactive sessions let user review and continue
- One-window-per-worktree model preserved (new tab for additional actions)

**Cons:**
- Each action button opens a *new* tab/window with a fresh Claude/Copilot session (not reusing an existing session)
- GitHub doesn't provide resolved/unresolved comment distinction — button logic differs by provider
- Users must have `claude` or `copilot` CLI installed and on PATH

**Implementation Considerations:**
- The `openNewTabInWindow` function currently uses `wt.exe -w 0 new-tab` which targets the *focused* window. We need to ensure we focus the tracked HWND first (which it already does at line 87-88), then open the tab.
- Prompt escaping is critical — PR URLs contain special characters. The existing `escapedPrompt.Replace("'", "''")`  handles single quotes for PowerShell.
- For "No PR" action, the prompt needs to be a meaningful instruction like: `"Commit all changes, push to origin, and create a pull request for this branch"`

## Impact Assessment

- **Scope:** Server (SessionManager, WorktreeApi, Types), Client (App.fs, index.html)
- **Risk Level:** Low — extends existing infrastructure, no changes to core refresh loop or state management
- **Breaking Changes:** No — new API endpoints and UI elements only
- **Testing Requirements:**
  - Unit: prompt construction, provider-aware command building
  - E2E: button visibility based on PR/build state, disabled state when coding tool active
  - Manual: actual terminal spawn/tab with coding tool launch (Explicit tests)

## Verification Strategy

- E2E tests for button visibility: buttons appear only when conditions are met (unresolved comments, failed build, no PR)
- E2E tests for disabled state: buttons disabled when coding tool status is Working/WaitingForUser
- Manual spawn tests (Explicit): verify terminal opens with correct coding tool and prompt
- Verify existing session management still works (focus, kill, new tab)

## Next Steps

1. Use this investigation as input to `/bd-plan` for implementation planning
2. Key implementation order:
   - Extend server: `SessionManager` tab-with-command, new API endpoint
   - Add shared types: `LaunchInTabRequest` or extend `LaunchRequest`
   - Add client messages and update handler
   - Add action buttons to PR row and build badges
   - Add "No PR" action button
   - CSS for new button styles
   - E2E tests for button visibility and disabled states

input.content:
# Contextual Action Buttons

## Goals

- Launch a coding tool (Claude/Copilot) in interactive mode directly from dashboard card badges
- Target three specific workflows: fix unresolved PR comments, fix failing builds, create a PR
- Reuse existing session management — spawn window if none exists, open new tab if tracked window exists
- Buttons disabled when coding tool is already active (Working/WaitingForUser)

## Expected Behavior

### Action Buttons

Three contextual action buttons appear on cards when conditions are met:

| Condition | Button Location | Prompt Sent |
|-----------|----------------|-------------|
| Unresolved PR comments (AzDo: `unresolved > 0`, GitHub: `total > 0`) | Next to thread/comment badge | `/pr <pr-url>` |
| Failed build | Next to failed build badge | `/fix-build <build-url>` |
| No PR exists | PR row area (where badge would be) | `Commit all changes, push to origin, and create a pull request for this branch` |

### Disabled State

All action buttons disabled when `CodingTool = Working` or `CodingTool = WaitingForUser`, matching the sync button pattern. Tooltip shows "{provider} is active".

### Session Handling

Single smart API endpoint (`launchAction`) handles both cases:
- **No tracked window**: Spawns new Windows Terminal window with coding tool + prompt (reuses `launchSession` path)
- **Tracked window exists**: Focuses window, opens new tab with coding tool + prompt

### Interactive Mode

Sessions must stay open for user to review and interact:
- Claude: `claude "<prompt>"` (positional argument, not `-p`)
- Copilot: `copilot -i "<prompt>"` (interactive flag, not `-p`)

## Technical Approach

### Server: Smart Launch Endpoint

New `launchAction` API endpoint on `IWorktreeApi`:
```
launchAction: LaunchRequest -> Async<Result<unit, string>>
```

Takes existing `LaunchRequest = { Path: WorktreePath; Prompt: string }`. Implementation checks `SessionManager` for tracked HWND:
- Found + valid → focus window, open new tab with provider-aware command
- Not found → spawn new window with provider-aware command

### Server: Provider-Aware Command Construction

`SessionManager.openNewTabInWindow` extended to accept an optional command string. New helper builds the interactive launch command per provider:
- Reads `CodingToolProvider` from `CodingToolStatus` for the worktree
- Constructs `claude '<escaped>'` or `copilot -i '<escaped>'`
- Falls back to Claude when no provider detected

### Client: Single Msg + Button Functions

One new `Msg` variant: `LaunchAction of path: WorktreePath * prompt: string`. Update handler calls `worktreeApi.launchAction`.

Action button rendering functions take `dispatch`, `wt: WorktreeStatus`, and condition-specific data (PR URL, build URL). Each checks `wt.CodingTool` for disabled state.

## Decisions

- **Single smart endpoint over separate spawn/tab endpoints**: Client doesn't need to know session state — server checks HWND and picks the right path
- **Positional argument over stdin/SendInput**: `claude "prompt"` starts interactive mode reliably; no stdin pipe exists to wt.exe-launched processes
- **Disabled over hidden**: Action buttons show as disabled (not hidden) when coding tool is active — user sees the actions exist but understands why they can't click

## Key Files

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `src/Shared/Types.fs` | Add `launchAction` to `IWorktreeApi` |
| `src/Server/SessionManager.fs` | Extend `openNewTabInWindow` with optional command, add `LaunchAction` message |
| `src/Server/WorktreeApi.fs` | Wire `launchAction` endpoint with provider-aware command construction |
| `src/Client/App.fs` | Add `LaunchAction` msg, action button rendering functions, integrate into PR row and build badges |
| `src/Client/index.html` | CSS for action button styles |

## Related Specs

- `docs/spec/native-session-management.md` — Session management foundation, explicitly designed for "future contextual actions"
- `docs/spec/worktree-monitor.md` — Card layout, PR/build badge rendering

toolUseResult.content:
# Contextual Action Buttons

## Goals

- Launch a coding tool (Claude/Copilot) in interactive mode directly from dashboard card badges
- Target three specific workflows: fix unresolved PR comments, fix failing builds, create a PR
- Reuse existing session management — spawn window if none exists, open new tab if tracked window exists
- Buttons disabled when coding tool is already active (Working/WaitingForUser)

## Expected Behavior

### Action Buttons

Three contextual action buttons appear on cards when conditions are met:

| Condition | Button Location | Prompt Sent |
|-----------|----------------|-------------|
| Unresolved PR comments (AzDo: `unresolved > 0`, GitHub: `total > 0`) | Next to thread/comment badge | `/pr <pr-url>` |
| Failed build | Next to failed build badge | `/fix-build <build-url>` |
| No PR exists | PR row area (where badge would be) | `Commit all changes, push to origin, and create a pull request for this branch` |

### Disabled State

All action buttons disabled when `CodingTool = Working` or `CodingTool = WaitingForUser`, matching the sync button pattern. Tooltip shows "{provider} is active".

### Session Handling

Single smart API endpoint (`launchAction`) handles both cases:
- **No tracked window**: Spawns new Windows Terminal window with coding tool + prompt (reuses `launchSession` path)
- **Tracked window exists**: Focuses window, opens new tab with coding tool + prompt

### Interactive Mode

Sessions must stay open for user to review and interact:
- Claude: `claude "<prompt>"` (positional argument, not `-p`)
- Copilot: `copilot -i "<prompt>"` (interactive flag, not `-p`)

## Technical Approach

### Server: Smart Launch Endpoint

New `launchAction` API endpoint on `IWorktreeApi`:
```
launchAction: LaunchRequest -> Async<Result<unit, string>>
```

Takes existing `LaunchRequest = { Path: WorktreePath; Prompt: string }`. Implementation checks `SessionManager` for tracked HWND:
- Found + valid → focus window, open new tab with provider-aware command
- Not found → spawn new window with provider-aware command

### Server: Provider-Aware Command Construction

`SessionManager.openNewTabInWindow` extended to accept an optional command string. New helper builds the interactive launch command per provider:
- Reads `CodingToolProvider` from `CodingToolStatus` for the worktree
- Constructs `claude '<escaped>'` or `copilot -i '<escaped>'`
- Falls back to Claude when no provider detected

### Client: Single Msg + Button Functions

One new `Msg` variant: `LaunchAction of path: WorktreePath * prompt: string`. Update handler calls `worktreeApi.launchAction`.

Action button rendering functions take `dispatch`, `wt: WorktreeStatus`, and condition-specific data (PR URL, build URL). Each checks `wt.CodingTool` for disabled state.

## Decisions

- **Single smart endpoint over separate spawn/tab endpoints**: Client doesn't need to know session state — server checks HWND and picks the right path
- **Positional argument over stdin/SendInput**: `claude "prompt"` starts interactive mode reliably; no stdin pipe exists to wt.exe-launched processes
- **Disabled over hidden**: Action buttons show as disabled (not hidden) when coding tool is active — user sees the actions exist but understands why they can't click

## Key Files

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `src/Shared/Types.fs` | Add `launchAction` to `IWorktreeApi` |
| `src/Server/SessionManager.fs` | Extend `openNewTabInWindow` with optional command, add `LaunchAction` message |
| `src/Server/WorktreeApi.fs` | Wire `launchAction` endpoint with provider-aware command construction |
| `src/Client/App.fs` | Add `LaunchAction` msg, action button rendering functions, integrate into PR row and build badges |
| `src/Client/index.html` | CSS for action button styles |

content.text:
Now for verification strategy. This feature has two distinct aspects to verify:

**What can be E2E tested (Playwright):**
- Button visibility: action buttons appear/hide based on PR status, build status, comment counts
- Disabled state: buttons disabled when coding tool is Working/WaitingForUser
- Button presence in correct locations (next to badges, in PR row area)

**What requires manual testing:**
- Actual terminal spawn with coding tool and prompt (requires real Windows Terminal, Claude/Copilot CLI)
- Tab opening in existing tracked window
- Interactive session staying open after prompt

content.text: Now let me run the plan improver.

## Improvements Applied

- **Fixed spec inaccuracy** in `Q:\code\tm-actions\docs\spec\contextual-actions.md`: Changed "Reads CodingToolProvider from CodingToolStatus" to "Reads CodingToolProvider from per-worktree state (CodingToolData map in PerRepoState)". `CodingToolStatus` is a DU (`Working|WaitingForUser|Done|Idle`), not a container for provider info. The provider is stored separately in the `CodingToolData` map as a `CodingToolProvider option`.

- **Added unit tests to server task** (`tm-actions-dgw`): The provider-aware command builder is a pure function (`Claude -> 'claude "{prompt}"'`, `Copilot -> 'copilot -i "{prompt}"'`) that should be unit tested. Added step 7 covering Claude format, Copilot format, fallback to Claude when provider is None, and prompt escaping with special characters.

- **Clarified provider lookup location** in server task (`tm-actions-dgw`): Step 6 now explicitly states "look up CodingToolProvider from CodingToolData in PerRepoState, pass to SessionManager". Previously the task was ambiguous about where provider resolution happens.

- **Removed duplicate Types.fs mention** from client task (`tm-actions-97o`): The old description had step 6 saying "Add launchAction to IWorktreeApi in Types.fs" which is already in the server task (`tm-actions-dgw`, step 5). Removed to avoid confusion about which task owns that change.

- **Simplified E2E test task** (`tm-actions-md9`): Replaced 9 specific positive/negative assertions (many of which depend on specific data conditions existing in live data) with 7 structural assertions that gracefully skip when expected data conditions aren't present. Per the project's testing guidelines: "assert on CSS classes and DOM structure, not specific data values."

- **Refined manual spawn test task** (`tm-actions-cb0`): Removed test case 4 ("Verify provider-aware command: Claude vs Copilot") since command construction is a pure function better covered by unit tests in the server task. Added note clarifying this split. Kept 4 tests focused on actual spawn/window behavior that can only be verified manually.

## Questions for User

1. **Create-PR action on all NoPr cards**: The spec shows a "Create PR" action button when `PrStatus = NoPr`. This would include the main branch worktree, which typically has no PR. Should the create-PR button be suppressed on the main/master branch, or is it acceptable to show it everywhere (relying on the user's judgment not to click it on main)?

2. **GitHub comment threshold**: For AzDo, the PR comments action shows when `unresolved > 0` (clear signal: there are threads needing resolution). For GitHub, the spec uses `total > 0` (any comments at all). This means the action button would appear even if all GitHub comments are positive approvals. Is `total > 0` the intended threshold for GitHub, or should there be a higher bar (e.g., only show when review state is "changes requested")?
